name: Joe LaBarbera
abstract: Joseph James LaBarbera (born February 22, 1948) is an American jazz drummer and composer. He is best known for his recordings and live performances with the trio of pianist Bill Evans in the final years of Evans's career.

interviews.title: Joe LaBarbera interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 15, 2021

concerts.label: A Chuck Mangione Concert with Concert Orchestra

works.t: Ziegfeld Follies of 1927: Shaking the Blues Away

about_quotes.speaker: Clay Jenkins
about_quotes.evidence: I play a lot with this great drummer... Joe LaBarbera... I love playing with him... Clay maintains a robust performing schedule and records with jazz artists such as... Joe LaBarbera.
about_quotes.doc_title: Clay Jenkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 18, 2016

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: And the drummer was Joe LaBarbera, who wound up also with Bill Evans
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Curtis Fuller
about_quotes.evidence: Actually, a jammer had to play the part for Buddy Rich so he can hear what it was supposed to sound like, Joe LaBarbera. He traveled with the band to rehearse the band somebody could hear it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Curtis Fuller — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Soph
about_quotes.evidence: Joe LaBarbera, who used to rehearse new charts for the band — Buddy would sit out front and Joe’d play it down once and Buddy’d say, “Let me try that.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Soph interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 9, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Mondello
about_quotes.evidence: Joe LaBarbera, who I know you know and you interviewed all three of my dear brothers, he did a book with Charles Levin on Bill Evans.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Mondello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 19, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Danko
about_quotes.evidence: Joe LaBarbera, who had gotten me on the gig... I looked at Joe LaBarbara and it was like okay, you’ll be on the gig I think
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Danko interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 28, 2021
